Raised in NY. APMA Sc. B. from Brown. Interested in probability theory, statistics, and machine learning. Currently working in quantitative finance.

You can contact me at timothy_sudijono@brown.edu. Blueno image above courtesy of S. Nadimpalli.

bio

2019-Current: Quant Portfolio Management, AQR Capital, Greenwich, CT. Equities Long Short.

2015-2019: Sc. B. APMA, Brown University
     (Honors, Phi Beta Kappa, Rohn Truell Prize)

Summer'18: Intern, AQR Capital, Greenwich, CT. Equities Long Only.

Summer'17: Student Researcher, ICERM, Providence, RI. Topological Data Analysis.

research

At Brown, I did research in probability theory, topological data analysis, and applied bayesian statistics. I was very fortunate to be mentored by Kavita Ramanan, Jose Perea, and Lorin Crawford.

  1. Periodicity Detection of Sampled Functions via Topological Analysis of Dynamic Networks
    (w/ C. Tralie, J. Perea)
    In Preparation, 2020
  2. A Statistical Pipeline for Identifying Physical Features that Differentiate Classes of 3D Shapes
    (w/ B. Wang, H. Kirveslahti, T. Gao, D. Boyer, S. Mukherjee, L. Crawford)
    Submitted - In Revision, 2019 bioRxiv:701391
  3. Stationarity and Ergodicity of Local Dynamics of Interacting Markov Chains on Large Sparse Graphs
    (advised by K. Ramanan, A. Ganguly)
    Sc. B. Thesis - Not Refereed, May 2019.
